author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xvii. 1, ‘After six days Jesus taketh with Him Peter, and James, and John his brother,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xvii. 1, ‘After six days Jesus taketh with Him Peter, and James, and John his brother,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We must now look into and treat of that vision which the Lord showed on the mount.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is this of which He had said, “Verily I say unto you, there be some standing here which shall not taste of death till they see the Son of Man in His Kingdom.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then began the passage which has just been read.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “When He had said this, after six days He took three disciples, Peter, and James, and John, and went up into a mountain.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These three were those “some,” of whom He had said, “There be some here which shall not taste of death, till they see the Son of Man in His kingdom.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is no small difficulty here.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that mount was not the whole extent of His kingdom.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is a mountain to Him who possesseth the heavens?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Which we not only read He doth, but in some sort see it with the eyes of the heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He calleth that His kingdom, which in many places He calleth the “kingdom of heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now the kingdom of heaven is the kingdom of the saints.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For the heavens declare the glory of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And of these heavens it is immediately said in the Psalm, “There is no speech nor language where their voice is not heard.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Their sound is gone out through all the earth, and their words unto the end of the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whose words, but of the heavens?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And of the Apostles, and all faithful preachers of the word of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These heavens therefore shall reign together with Him who made the heavens.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now consider what was done, that this might be made manifest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord Jesus Himself shone bright as the sun; His raiment became white as the snow; and Moses and Elias talked with Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Jesus Himself indeed shone as the sun, signifying that “He is the light which lighteth every man that cometh into the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What this sun is to the eyes of the flesh, that is He to the eyes of the heart; and what that is to the flesh of men, that is He to their hearts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now His raiment is His Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if the raiment be not held together by him who puts it on, it will fall off.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of this raiment, Paul was as it were a sort of last border.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he says himself, “I am the least of the Apostles.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And in another place, “I am the last of the Apostles.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now in a garment the border is the last and least part.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore as that woman which suffered from an issue of blood, when she had touched the Lord’s border was made whole, so the Church which came from out of the Gentiles, was made whole by the preaching of Paul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What wonder if the Church is signified by white raiment, when you hear the Prophet Isaiah saying, “Though your sins be as scarlet, I will make them white as snow”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Moses and Elias, that is, the Law and the Prophets, what avail they, except they converse with the Lord?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Except they give witness to the Lord, who would read the Law or the Prophets?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Mark how briefly the Apostle expresses this; “For by the Law is the knowledge of sin; but now the righteousness of God without the Law is manifested:” behold the sun; “being witnessed by the Law and the Prophets,” behold the shining of the Sun.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Peter sees this, and as a man savouring the things of men says, “Lord, it is good for us to be here.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He had been wearied with the multitude, he had found now the mountain’s solitude; there he had Christ the Bread of the soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What! should he depart thence again to travail and pains, possessed of a holy love to Godward, and thereby of a good conversation?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He wished well for himself; and so he added, “If Thou wilt, let us make here three tabernacles; one for Thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elias.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To this the Lord made no answer; but notwithstanding Peter was answered.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For while he yet spake, a bright cloud came, and overshadowed them.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He desired three tabernacles; the heavenly answer showed him that we have One, which human judgment desired to divide.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ, the Word of God, the Word of God in the Law, the Word in the Prophets.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why, Peter, dost thou seek to divide them?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It were more fitting for thee to join them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou seekest three; understand that they are but One.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As the cloud then overshadowed them, and in a way made one tabernacle for them, “a voice also sounded out of the cloud, which said, This is My beloved Son.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Moses was there; Elias was there; yet it was not said, “These are My beloved sons.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Only Son is one thing; adopted sons another.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was singled out in whom the Law and the prophets glorified.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “This is My beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased; hear Him!”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because ye have heard Him in the Prophets, and ye have heard Him in the Law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And where have ye not heard Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “When they heard this, they fell” to the earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See then in the Church is exhibited to us the Kingdom of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here is the Lord, here the Law and the Prophets; but the Lord as the Lord; the Law in Moses, Prophecy in Elias; only they as servants and as ministers.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They as vessels: He as the fountain: Moses and the Prophets spake, and wrote; but when they poured out, they were filled from Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the Lord stretched out His hand, and raised them as they lay.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And then “they saw no man, save Jesus only.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What does this mean?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the Apostle was being read, you heard, “For now we see through a glass darkly, but then face to face.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And “tongues shall cease,” when that which we now hope for and believe shall come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In then that they fell to the earth, they signified that we die, for it was said to the flesh, “Earth thou art, and unto earth shalt thou return.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when the Lord raised them up, He signified the resurrection.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: After the resurrection, what is the Law to thee? what Prophecy?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore neither Moses nor Elias is seen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He only remaineth to thee, “Who in the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He remaineth to thee, “that God may be all in all.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Moses will be there; but now no more the Law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We shall see Elias there too; but now no more the Prophet.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Law and the Prophets have only given witness to Christ, that it behoved Him to suffer, and to rise again from the dead the third day, and to enter into His glory.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And in this glory is fulfilled what He hath promised to them that love Him, “He that loveth Me shall be loved of My Father, and I will love him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And as if it were said, What wilt Thou give him, seeing Thou wilt love him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And I will manifest Myself unto him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Great gift! great promise!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God doth not reserve for thee as a reward anything of His own, but Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O thou covetous one; why doth not what Christ promiseth suffice thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou dost seem to thyself to be rich; yet if thou have not God, what hast thou?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Another is poor, yet if he hath God, what hath he not?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Come down, Peter: thou wast desiring to rest on the mount; come down, “preach the word, be instant in season, out of season, reprove, rebuke, exhort with all longsuffering and doctrine.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Endure, labour hard, bear thy measure of torture; that thou mayest possess what is meant by the white raiment of the Lord, through the brightness and the beauty of an upright labouring in charity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when the Apostle was being read we heard in praise of charity, “She seeketh not her own.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She seeketh not her own;” since she gives what she possesses.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In another place there is more danger in the expression, if you do not understand it right.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Apostle, charging the faithful members of Christ after this rule of charity, says, “Let no man seek his own, but another’s.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For on hearing this, covetousness is ready with its deceits, that in a matter of business under pretence of seeking another’s, it may defraud a man, and so, “seek not his own, but another’s.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But let covetousness restrain itself, let justice come forth; so let us hear and understand.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is to charity that it is said, “Let no man seek his own, but another’s.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now, O thou covetous one, if thou wilt still resist, and twist the precept rather to this point, that thou shouldest covet what is another’s; then lose what is thine own.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But as I know thee well, thou dost wish to have both thine own and another’s.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wilt commit fraud that thou mayest have what is another’s; submit then to robbery that thou mayest lose thine own.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou dost not wish to seek thine own, but then thou takest away what is another’s.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now this if thou do, thou doest not well.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear and listen, thou covetous one: the Apostle explains to thee in another place more clearly this that he said, “Let no man seek his own, but another’s.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He says of himself, “Not seeking mine own profit, but the profit of many, that they may be saved.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This Peter understood not yet when he desired to live on the mount with Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was reserving this for thee, Peter, after death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But now He saith Himself, “Come down, to labour in the earth; in the earth to serve, to be despised, and crucified in the earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Life came down, that He might be slain; the Bread came down, that He might hunger; the Way came down, that life might be wearied in the way; the Fountain came down, that He might thirst; and dost thou refuse to labour?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘Seek not thine own.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Have charity, preach the truth; so shall thou come to eternity, where thou shalt find security.”
